topic: AI learning app for non-technical busy professionals
description: Research focus: understand how non-technical, time-constrained professionals and career-switchers discover, evaluate, and abandon AI learning products — with emphasis on the language they use to describe overwhelm, comparison shopping, and desired outcomes like career advancement and side-hustle monetization.

search_queries: learn AI overwhelmed where to start
AI tools too many which one should I use
AI replace job what to do
best AI course beginner non technical
busy professional no time learn AI
ChatGPT tutorial beginner where to start
how to use ChatGPT at work
AI for non programmers non technical
best way to learn ChatGPT busy professional
AI replace job what to learn
learn AI overwhelmed where to start

apps.name: Coursiv
apps.notes: Pull all ratings. Tag 1-star and 5-star extremes for pain/benefit extraction.

extract_signals: comparison language (better than, worse than, switched from)

apps.name: Coursiv - AI Tools Mastery
apps.notes: Original app, most reviews. Main source.

apps.name: Coursiv - AI Certificates
apps.notes: Newer rebranded app. Pull for recent user language.

target_pages.priority: primary — pull ALL ads for Phase 1A
target_pages.notes: Our own product. Pull every ad to understand what they are testing.
Adjacent competitor — creative and professional skills
Adjacent competitor — professional upskilling with certificate positioning
Competitor — AI-specific courses, may skew technical but overlaps on prompt engineering
Large adjacent competitor — explicitly named in Coursiv seed vocabulary
Niche competitor — prompt engineering positioning overlaps directly with Coursiv benefit vocabulary
Non-technical AI learning positioning — direct ICP overlap
AI business skills learning app — overlaps on entrepreneur/side-hustle segment

extract_signals: hook (first line of copy or first 3 seconds)
format (UGC / talking head / screen recording / static / carousel / animation)
offer structure (free trial / discount / guarantee / social proof)

performance_filter.notes: Ads running 14+ days are likely profitable. Sort by duration descending. Country = ALL per TA requirement. Strategy output will filter to US audience lens.

llm_classification.custom_prompt_context: You are analyzing user-generated content (Reddit posts, YouTube comments, app reviews, and social media text) for a competitive intelligence project focused on AI learning products targeting non-technical, time-constrained professionals. For each piece of content, extract the following: (1) CONSIDERATION SET — list every learning product, app, platform, or course mentioned by name, and tag each with sentiment: recommended / complained_about / compared / dismissed / neutral. Flag any mentions of Coursiv, Coursive, or Cursiv as highest priority. (2) VERBATIM PAIN LANGUAGE — copy exact phrases (do not paraphrase) that express overwhelm with AI tools, fear of being left behind, lack of time to learn, confusion about where to start, career anxiety, or feeling that AI is too technical. Match against seed vocabulary: overwhelmed, too many AI tools, where to start, left behind, falling behind, no time, busy professional, career change, replace my job, non technical, without coding. (3) VERBATIM BENEFIT LANGUAGE — copy exact phrases expressing desired outcomes: career boost, work smarter, side hustle income, AI certificate, prompt engineering mastery, using AI at work, hiring advantage in 2026. (4) AUDIENCE SEGMENT — classify the author into exactly one of: busy_knowledge_worker_28_50, career_switcher_45_plus, aspiring_entrepreneur_sidehustler, remote_worker_freelancer, or absolute_beginner_curious_explorer. Use context clues (job title, age mentions, stated goals, subreddit context) to justify the classification. (5) CONTENT CATEGORY — assign one primary category from: frustration_time_constraints, technical_barrier_complaint, beginner_question, success_story, platform_comparison, career_transition_anxiety, course_recommendation, abandonment_regret, consideration_set_signal. Prioritize extracting direct quotes over summaries. Every extracted insight must include the source URL or review ID, the platform, and the verbatim text that triggered the classification.

_derivation.note: All queries derived from Phase 1 search_seeds.json. App IDs hardcoded from Phase 1 web research.
